compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic collapse of vegetative matter. The registry-defined specimens are no longer visible or have withered to the point of non-recognition.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot is empty; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant life.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot shows no signs of mint; surface is dark and featureless.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos specimen has completely vanished or decomposed.
plant_audit.P4: No evidence of the Silver Guest; pot surface is bare.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, potentially hydrophobic or overly saturated; no signs of active growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshells (calcium intervention) remains in the black pot, but no biological activity is detected.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: A small electronic component (likely a header pin connector) appeared in the T-4 image and was removed by T-2, indicating human intervention in the workspace.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-5: Initial state shows minimal foliage; Pothos leaf visible.
temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Gradual loss of leaf turgor and eventual disappearance of the Pothos leaf.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_current: Total loss of all visible plant structures; the biome has transitioned to a dormant/dead state.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered plants have suffered total mortality or removal. The environment is now just a collection of empty pots and inert soil.
anomalies: Introduction of electronic debris (header pins) in T-4.
Presence of eggshell fragments as a failed soil amendment.
Complete disappearance of all plant matter over the 5-day observation window.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a rapid decline of the indoor botanical collection. Starting from a state of minimal health, the specimens have progressively withered or been removed. By the current timestamp, the pots are entirely devoid of life, suggesting either a failure in care protocols or a deliberate clearing of the biome. The presence of non-biological debris suggests the desk is being used for secondary purposes, further compromising the environment for plant life.
